title: Phenomenon 77
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:O is for Oneirodynia}}
{{Background|file=Void.png}} {{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{Background|file=void.png}}__NOTOC__ {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=?|effectiveness=5|danger=0||width=}}
'''Oneriodynia''' /oʊˌnaɪrəˈdɪniə/ '''Noun'''
1. A violent or disturbed imagination during sleep, as in the case of nightmares and sleepwalking.
==Overview== Phenomenon 77, known as '''Oneiroprojection''' or '''Interdimensional Oneirodynia'''Terms originating from the Ancient Greek ''oneiron'', a word meaning "dream"., is a phenomenon affecting one's dreams. Unusually among anomalous events, Oneiroprojection only affects those within [[The Frontrooms]], with there being no record of an occurrence of it in The Backrooms, although some have attempted to classify similar phenomena such as those on [[Level 18]] or [[Level 125]] as Backrooms-based occurrences of it. Due to this, the phenomenon is difficult to directly study, due to those who are affected by it being unable to be contacted by researchers, leading to most information about it being sourced from the accounts of wanderers who previously experienced it and from posts on Frontrooms blogs and social media accessed through [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]]. During Oneiroprojection, one will experience a dream about a level of The Backrooms, referred to as a '''projection'''. No single level seems to have a greater change of appearing in a dream, although levels that resemble commonly seen locations in The Frontrooms or those with a "dreamcore" aesthetic appear more frequently, however this may just be because these are more compatible with ordinary dreams. One typically does not have control or lucidity during the dream, although individuals skilled at lucid dreaming may possess some form of control over the dream. Dreams containing Oneiroprojection have been reported to begin as normal, and typically contain a positive or nostalgic memory from one's past, typically from when they were a child or young adult. Many have reported having similar, non-anomalous dreams prior to experiencing a projection not featuring elements relating to The Backrooms. During the dream the dreamer would begin to notice minor inconsistencies in the memory, such as the presence of foreign objects or certain things being colored differently. Eventually, more noticeable changes will begin to be seen, such as [[Phenomenon 11|reality being warped and distorted]] and people [[Entity 9|appearing without faces]]. The perspective of the dream would then shift outward away from the scene that is occurring and toward one's surroundings, which would begin shifting from familiar terrain to that of the level the projection is taking place in as the dreamer departs from the initial memory. Dreamers have reported that during projections, they experience elements of the level, such entities and structural effects caused by [[Phenomenon 11|Instability]]. Appearances of human wanderers are also occasionally reported, although specific information about the wanderers is not clear enough to determine whether these correspond to actual people. Entities and wanderers have shown no reaction to the presence of the dreamer, who is akin to an invisible observer from their perspective. The one exception to this is the presence of the [[Juju MAn]], who seems to be able to perceive dreamers as he does with those in the Backrooms. ==Theories== Due to the enigmatic nature of Oneiroprojection, a variety of theories have been formulated attempting to explain the phenomenon. ===Astral Projection theory=== The Astral Projection theory states that Oneiroprojection is a form of astral projection, a phenomena reported in various esoteric traditions involving one's soul or spirit leaving their body and ascending to a supernatural plane of existence. Wanderers who subscribe to these traditions have made claims that The Backrooms is one of the said astral realms, and that Oneiroprojection is a form of astral projection. Others claim the opposite, stating that accounts of astral projection are actually instances of Oneiroprojection interpreted as a spiritual experience. ===Subconscious theory=== The Subconscious Theory, also known as the Inverse [[The Kauer Thesis|Kauer Thesis]], hypothesizes that the Backrooms is innately linked to the human subconscious, with the Backrooms subtly influencing humanity throughout history, explaining the similarities between The Backrooms and the unconscious. Advocates of this theory claim that Oneiroprojection is elements of the Backrooms found in the subconscious entering ordinary dreams rather than one actually observing the Backrooms during a projection. ==Recovered Documents== |-|File 1=
I had a weird dream last night. I was in [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Level_30 an indoor waterpark], a crummy old one I went to when I was a kid. What was its name again? The dream started with me climbing up a flight of stairs to a water slide. They say you can't smell in dreams, but that smell of a little too much chlorine was in the air. Then things got weird. I took the slide, but instead of ending up in the pool at the bottom, it spat me out in a big room that looked kind of like a spa with pools, except there was junk and debris everywhere, and the water looked nasty and dark. Then I saw a woman lying in the corner. She looked [[Hydrolitis Plague|very pale and sickly]], and was coughing out a bunch of blood. I don't think she saw me, even though I was standing right in front of her, and I had the feeling there was nothing I could do to help. I woke up then. I heard dreams reflect your subconscious, and if that is true, I feel like this one might be important.
[[File:Level 30 Orcus-Sedna Area.jpg|center|300px]]
-Excerpt from online blog
|-|File 2=
Last night, I had a dream that reminded me of my childhood. I was inside [[Level 859|an indoor market]] that was near my apartment, going to get some groceries. Everything was going normal, but when I turned to enter the produce section, everything changed. Instead of being crowded with people like before, the market was empty, and the food looked rotten and putrid, with flies swarming all over it. I then saw some movement behind a food cart. Looking to investigate, I saw it was not a person, but a [[Entity 6|nightmarish being]] with a face like melted wax and too-long arms bending in unnatural ways. Luckily, it did not seem to be able to see me, which would have given me a chance to run away if it wasn't for a giant gust of wind and flow of murky water, like those during an especially bad typhoon season, knocking me down. Just as I was about to be submerged by the water, I woke up in bed, covered in sweat.
[[File:Fish and Produce found together.jpg|center|300px]]
-Post on internet chat room, translated from Filipino
|-|File 3=
I always liked the ocean. As a little kid, I went down to the beach and played in the waves nearly every day in the summer. When I was older, I learned how to surf, and became interested in fishing. Now, I still go on walks by the shore now and then. But the experiences of my childhood have always been the ones that brought the most joy to me, which may be why I still dream of them. However, one night, the dream was different. It started as usual, happy memories of building sandcastles and running in the surf. But something changed. It began to get dark, and everyone else seemed to leave the beach, leaving me behind. I called out for my parents, but nobody came. It was then that I realized that I had lost sight of the ocean, my surroundings being only [[Level 135|a dark expanse of sand]], covered in abandoned beach chairs. I then had the sudden feeling that there was something big, something malicious lurking under the sand, a sense of dread travelling through my whole body. Then, things got even stranger. I saw [[Juju Man|this man]], dressed in all gray, with a long coat and hat. He didn't particularly look out of the ordinary, but something about his presence radiated pure, unearthly fear. It's hard to describe, but seeing him was like looking into an infinite void that could consume me at any second. He looked right in my face, and said "Leave now, or you will learn of a true nightmare". I immediately woke up, hyperventilating. Whether I write it down or not, i'll remember that dream for the rest of my life.
[[File:SandscapeChairs2.jpg|center|300px]]
-Unknown Source
|-|File 4=
It has been a long time since I was young, and the memories of my youth have long since faded away. When I rack my brain now, only vague recollections and half-formed images come to me, warmth and color that have long since faded to dust. However, in the back of my mind, in my deepest dreams they remain, bright as day. For a brief time, I can forget the weight of many decades and return to the innocence of childhood. However, one such dream I had tonight was different than the rest, and shattered my view of the nature of dreams and memory. It began as the others had. I was 11 or 12 years old, walking through [[Level 85|the bustling downtown]] of the city in which I was raised with my mother and younger brother. I gazed at the newly installed billboards adorned with lights. As we walked past a convenience store, I saw something in the window that gave me a double take. It was a familiar advertisement, featuring a man holding a glass of Budweiser. One glaring detail was different: the man had [[Entity 9|no face]]. As soon as I had this realization, my vision briefly flashed a bright orange, as if I was looking at an overexposed polaroid in an old photo album. At the same moment, all the clamor of the city street had abruptly ceased. When I looked behind me, I found that the streets were devoid of all activity, vehicles stopped in their places. Not knowing what to do, I began walking down the street. The streets were fully empty, with not even the wind rustling the trees. It was as if the whole world had stopped in time, a still frame frozen in an eternal past. But something was wrong, as if the otherwise perfect snapshot had been tampered with. Nuclear sirens littered the area, poles haphazardly spread across the streets and jutting out of buildings, counting down in an alien language. I had the sense that the countdown was leading up to something sinister and vile lurking below the facade of nostalgia. Half-lucid, the countdown ringing in my ears, I desperately willed myself to wake up, and was suddenly snapped out of my nightmare. To this day, I still do not know what was infiltrating my dreams, an outside force or simply the darkness of my past my old mind had pushed aside.
[[File:Level-85-?.png|center|300px]]
-Taken from the diary of an unknown individual found buried in the sand in [[Level 100]]
|-|File 5=
I used to live next to an old furniture store, back before my family moved to Minneapolis for that job. Some friends of my parents bought the store, [[Senseless|hired some guys to take out all the old furniture]] and began renovating. I think they ended up building a kind of hobby shop. When they weren't working, they let me and my friends play in the unrenovated hallways of the place. I never told anyone this, but the place always creeped me out when I was alone. Something about those peeling yellow walls and buzzing lights just felt weird or wrong. The feeling must have stuck with me, because years later, I had a dream about that place. It started with me entering the building, going beyond the renovated areas into the old back rooms. It was there where I saw a door, made of rotting wood, that had never been there before. I opened it and walked through. The place I entered looked just like the rooms before, the same beige carpet and yellowing walls, but in a layout I had never seen before. I kept walking, and when I looked behind me, I saw [[Shifting Geometry|the door had disappeared]], replaced by more unfamiliar rooms. As I walked on, I had a feeling that the unease I had felt in this place, If it even was the same place was right, and that I should have turned back when I had the chance. Seeing only one option in front of me, I walked farther into the rooms, the buzzing and awful carpet smell overpowering my senses. After what felt like hours of walking deeper and deeper, I woke up, not feeling well-rested at all. When I woke up one day on [[Level 0|that same yellowish floor]], I thought I was having another nightmare. And I'm still waiting to wake up.
[[File:Thebackrooms.jpg|center|300px]]
-Testimony of an anonymous wanderer, interviewed in [[Level 154]]
